name: Bumpy Highway Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Spray a 10-inch Bundt pan with vegetable oil spray.
3. Measure 1/3 cup sweetened condensed milk for the cake; reserve the remaining for frosting.
4. In a large bowl, combine shortening, eggs, granulated sugar, brown sugar, and 1/3 cup condensed milk. Beat until creamy.
5. Add cocoa powder and buttermilk to the mixture; blend thoroughly.
6. In a separate bowl, mix flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt.
7. Add the dry ingredients to the creamed mixture, stirring to combine.
8. Stir in vanilla extract.
9. Beat at medium speed until well blended (do not overbeat). The batter will be fairly thin.
10. Pour batter into prepared Bundt pan.
11. Bake for 35 to 50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean and the cake springs back when lightly touched.
12. Cool in the pan for 5 minutes before removing.
13. For the drizzle: Combine cocoa powder and vegetable oil; stir to blend. Drizzle over the serving plate and place the cake on the fluted side up.
14. In a medium bowl, combine reserved condensed milk with confectioners' sugar. Beat until glossy and of spreading consistency.
15. Spread frosting on warm cake, sprinkling with miniature marshmallows and chopped nuts.
16. Decorate with remaining chocolate drizzle. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Makes 1 10-inch Bundt cake (16 to 20 servings).
